summary: Patrick McKenzie writes a seasonal marketing post targeting Google searches for 'free Halloween bingo cards,' directing traffic to his Bingo Card Creator business. The post offers genuinely useful tips for parents and teachers on how to use Halloween bingo cards with children, including creative suggestions for markers and prizes. He closes with a transparent aside to his regular readers acknowledging the post is SEO-driven content. This is a textbook example of his content marketing philosophy: create useful content that naturally attracts search traffic to your product.
key_points: The post is a deliberate SEO play targeting seasonal search traffic for 'free Halloween bingo cards,' which was already sending hundreds of visitors daily
McKenzie provides genuinely useful content (activity suggestions for parents) rather than a thin sales pitch, demonstrating his content marketing approach
He uses a separate domain (halloweenbingocards.net) to capture niche seasonal traffic and funnel it toward his main product
The aside to regular readers reveals his transparent, analytical approach to marketing — he knows exactly why he's writing this post and isn't pretending otherwise
money_quotes: It is going to be Halloween shortly and Google is sending me hundreds of people looking for free Halloween bingo cards.
I am a former teacher, and we can be downright devious when it comes to incentivizing competition without crushing the spirits of the folks who don't end up first.
Apologies to my regular readers, but I was getting a hundred search hits a day. Back to your regularly scheduled content tomorrow.
key_insight: Creating genuinely useful seasonal content that matches high-volume search queries is a low-effort way to funnel organic traffic toward your product.
